shortAnswer: Beta-hCG is best known as a pregnancy hormone and rises sharply in the first weeks of pregnancy. Outside of pregnancy, it's used as a tumor marker for certain germ cell tumors. Interpretation depends heavily on the context.
metaDescription: Beta-hCG elevated? Meaning for pregnancy and as a tumor marker explained.
causesElevated: Germ cell tumors (rare, outside of pregnancy)
doctorQuestions: In what context was Beta-hCG measured for me?
